question_text: Outline a sequence of reactions that would bring about the conversion of aniline into benzylamine. The question includes chemical structures showing aniline (aminobenzene) converting to benzylamine (benzylaminomethane). The reaction sequence shows: 1) NaNO2/HCl to give the diazonium salt, 2) CuCN to give benzonitrile, 3) LiAlH4 reduction, work-up with OH- and H2O, or alternately H2 and catalyst to get benzylamine. Provide the full sequence of steps.
student_answer:
Step 1: Diazonium salt formation

Aniline is first nitrated by reacting with sodium nitrite (NaNO2) in the presence of hydrochloric acid (HCl). This reaction forms a diazonium salt.

Step 2: Benzonitrile formation

The diazonium salt is then reacted with copper cyanide (CuCN) in aqueous solution. This reaction forms benzonitrile.

Step 3: Benzylamine formation

Benzonitrile is then reduced to benzylamine. This reduction is carried out using lithium aluminum hydride (LiAlH4) in an aqueous solution. The reduction involves the addition of hydride ions (H-) to the nitrile group, resulting in the formation of benzylamine. After the reduction, the work-up involves neutralizing the excess hydride ions with hydroxide ions (OH-), followed by addition of water (H2O).
